name: Clootie Dumpling
recipeIngredient: 3 oz Shredded suet (usually available at your butcher)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix flour with suet, fruit, sugar, cinnamon, and baking soda.
2. Stir in enough buttermilk to make a soft batter.
3. Dip a pudding cloth (cheesecloth) into boiling water, then drain it well in a basin large enough to hold the batter.
4. Dredge the cloth lightly with flour, then spoon in the batter evenly.
5. Draw the fullness of the cloth together tightly and tie securely with string, leaving room for swelling.
6. Place a saucer or plate in the bottom of a large saucepan. Lift the dumpling into the pan.
7. Pour in enough boiling water to cover completely.
8. Simmer for 2 hours.
9. Untie the dumpling carefully and turn it out onto a hot serving dish.
10. Dredge with castor sugar.
11. Serve with hot custard sauce (see note).
Note: For the hot custard sauce, use Byrd's Custard if available in your area. It comes in a large tin similar to powdered chocolate for chocolate milk. Follow the directions to make a custard and dilute it slightly to create a sauce-like consistency.
